_source: Composed from LangChain's @tool decorator pattern and the LangGraph ToolNode interface. Tool schemas in LangGraph are Pydantic / TypedDict based; this file shows the canonical pattern users adopt when defining tools for a LangGraph agent.

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get current weather for a given location.

tools.name: search_database
tools.description: Run a parameterized read query against the customer database.

tools.name: send_email
tools.description: Send an email on behalf of the user.

tool_node.description: Prebuilt `ToolNode` from `langgraph.prebuilt` executes tool calls emitted by the LLM. It does not by itself add validation beyond the @tool decorator's Pydantic schema — validation happens inside the tool function or via Pydantic at the boundary.
tool_node.behavior_on_error: ToolNode catches tool execution exceptions and returns them as ToolMessage content to the LLM. The LLM can then decide to retry or replan. There is no built-in bounded-retry counter; the recursion_limit on the graph is the only hard backstop.
tool_node.parallel_tool_calls: Supported. The agent node may emit multiple tool calls in one LLM step; ToolNode dispatches them in parallel via the `Send` API (v2 streaming).

validation_notes.where_validation_happens: Pydantic schema validation happens when the tool is called — i.e. after the LLM has already emitted a tool call but before the function body executes. The @tool decorator wraps the function with the Pydantic model declared via args_schema.
what_is_NOT_validated_by_default: Foreign-key existence (e.g. does this order_id exist in the CRM)
Business-rule constraints (e.g. refund amount <= order amount)
Cross-tool consistency (e.g. tool A's output makes tool B's call invalid)
validation_notes.guidance: Users are expected to add these checks inside the tool function body. The framework does not provide a generic validation layer.

dry_run_mode.workaround: Use a checkpointer + interrupt_before=['tools'] to pause before tool execution, inspect the planned call, then resume with `graph.invoke(None, config)` to execute or `graph.update_state(...)` to modify before execution.

approval_gate.mechanism: interrupt_before=['tools'] or interrupt_after=['agent'] pauses the graph before tool execution. The orchestrator can present the planned tool call to a human, gather approval, and resume. This is documented as 'human-in-the-loop' in the LangGraph persistence guide.
